method_atom: A quantization scheme that takes a classically integrable system carrying infinitely many mutually commuting conserved quantities generated from an infinite-dimensional symmetry algebra, builds a corresponding family of commuting quantum operators, and conjectures that their common joint spectrum is encoded by differential operators bearing a spectral parameter (Schroedinger-type operators) attached to the dual symmetry algebra under a Langlands-type duality that exchanges an algebra with its dual; the correspondence between commuting quantum integrals and differential operators is thereby framed as a special case of that duality.
object_atom: Classical soliton integrable hierarchies of nonlinear wave equations — concretely the KdV hierarchy — together with their quantum integrals of motion, whose predicted spectra are expected to reproduce those of Bazhanov, Lukyanov and Zamolodchikov.
brief_reason: What carries across is the quantize-an-integrable-system-and-encode-its-spectrum-by-dual-differential-operators-via-a-Langlands-type-duality machinery, while the domain-specific object is the KdV/soliton hierarchy it acts on, though the method remains tightly bound to the representation theory of affine algebras.
